It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    50→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    51→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    52→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    53→ */\n    54→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    55→\n    56→    private final Time time;\n    57→    private final Logger log;\n    58→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    59→\n    60→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    61→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    62→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    63→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    64→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    65→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    66→\n    67→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    68→\n    69→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    70→        @Override\n    71→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    72→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→\n    76→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    77→                              final String clientId,\n    78→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    79→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    80→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    81→                              final int numExecutors\n    82→                              ) {\n    83→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    84→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    85→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    86→        this.time = time;\n    87→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    88→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    89→\n    90→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    91→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    92→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    93→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    94→        }\n    95→    }\n    96→\n    97→    @Override\n    98→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n    99→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   100→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   101→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   102→            }\n   103→\n   104→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   105→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   106→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   107→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   108→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   109→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   110→                ) {\n   111→\n   112→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   113→\n   114→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   115→\n   116→                    return task;\n   117→                }\n   118→            }\n   119→\n   120→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   121→\n   122→            return null;\n   123→        });\n   124→    }\n   125→\n   126→    @Override\n   127→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   128→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   129→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   130→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   131→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   132→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   133→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   134→                ) {\n   135→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   136→                    return false;\n   137→                }\n   138→            }\n   139→            try {\n   140→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   141→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   142→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   143→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   144→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   145→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   146→                } else {\n   147→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   148→                }\n   149→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   150→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   151→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   152→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   153→                return true;\n   154→            }\n   155→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   156→            return false;\n   157→        });\n   158→\n   159→        if (interrupted) {\n   160→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   161→        }\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   165→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   166→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    @Override\n   170→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   171→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   172→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   173→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for unassign task is unrecognized\");\n   174→            }\n   175→\n   176→            final TaskExecutor lockedExecutor = assignedTasks.get(task.id());\n   177→            if (lockedExecutor == null || lockedExecutor != executor) {\n   178→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Task \" + task.id() + \" is not locked by the executor\");\n   179→            }\n   180→\n   181→            assignedTasks.remove(task.id());\n   182→\n   183→            log.debug(\"Unassigned {} from executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   184→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   185→        });\n   186→    }\n   187→\n   188→    @Override\n   189→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   190→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> result = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n   191→\n   192→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   193→            result.complete(null);\n   194→            return result;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→            lockedTasks.addAll(taskIds);\n   199→\n   200→            final Set<TaskId> remainingTaskIds = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>(taskIds);\n   201→\n   202→            for (final TaskId taskId : taskIds) {\n   203→                final Task task = tasks.initializedTask(taskId);\n   204→\n   205→                if (task == null) {\n   206→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Trying to lock task \" + taskId + \" but it's not owned\");\n   207→                }\n   208→\n   209→                if (!task.isActive()) {\n   210→                    throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The locking task \" + taskId + \" is not an active task\");\n   211→                }\n   212→\n   213→                if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   214→                    final TaskExecutor executor = assignedTasks.get(taskId);\n   215→                    log.debug(\"Requesting release of task {} from {}\", taskId, executor.name());\n   216→                    final KafkaFuture<StreamTask> future = executor.unassign();\n   217→                    future.whenComplete((streamTask, throwable) -> {\n   218→                        if (throwable != null) {\n   219→                            result.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   220→                        } else {\n   221→                            assert !assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId);\n   222→                            // It can happen that the executor handed back the task before we asked it to\n   223→                            // in which case `streamTask` will be null here.\n   224→                            assert streamTask == null || streamTask.id() == taskId;\n   225→                            remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   226→                            if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   227→                                result.complete(null);\n   228→                            }\n   229→                        }\n   230→                    });\n   231→                } else {\n   232→                    remainingTaskIds.remove(taskId);\n   233→                    if (remainingTaskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   234→                        result.complete(null);\n   235→                    }\n   236→                }\n   237→            }\n   238→\n   239→            return result;\n   240→        });\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    @Override\n   244→    public KafkaFuture<Void> lockAllTasks() {\n   245→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() ->\n   246→            lockTasks(tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(Task::id).collect(Collectors.toSet()))\n   247→        );\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    @Override\n   251→    public void unlockTasks(final Set<TaskId> taskIds) {\n   252→\n   253→        if (taskIds.isEmpty()) {\n   254→            return;\n   255→        }\n   256→\n   257→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   258→            lockedTasks.removeAll(taskIds);\n   259→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   260→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   261→        });\n   262→    }\n   263→\n   264→    @Override\n   265→    public void unlockAllTasks() {\n   266→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> tasksToAdd) {\n   271→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   272→            for (final StreamTask task : tasksToAdd) {\n   273→                tasks.addActiveTask(task);\n   274→            }\n   275→            log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   276→            tasksCondition.signalAll();\n   277→        });\n   278→\n   279→        log.info(\"Added tasks {} to the task manager to process\", tasksToAdd);\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    @Override\n   283→    public void remove(final TaskId taskId) {\n   284→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   285→            if (assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   286→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is still assigned to executors\");\n   287→            }\n   288→\n   289→            if (!lockedTasks.contains(taskId)) {\n   290→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not locked yet by the task manager\");\n   291→            }\n   292→\n   293→            if (!tasks.containsInitialized(taskId)) {\n   294→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The task to remove is not owned by the task manager\");\n   295→            }\n   296→\n   297→            tasks.removeTask(tasks.initializedTask(taskId));\n   298→        });\n   299→\n   300→        log.info(\"Removed task {} from the task manager\", taskId);\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    @Override\n   304→    public Set<ReadOnlyTask> getTasks() {\n   305→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> tasks.activeInitializedTasks().stream().map(ReadOnlyTask::new).collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    @Override\n   309→    public void setUncaughtException(final StreamsException exception, final TaskId taskId) {\n   310→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   311→\n   312→            if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   313→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"An uncaught exception can only be set as long as the task is still assigned\");\n   314→            }\n   315→\n   316→            if (uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId)) {\n   317→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The uncaught exception must be cleared before restarting processing\");\n   318→            }\n   319→\n   320→            uncaughtExceptions.put(taskId, exception);\n   321→        });\n   322→\n   323→        log.info(\"Set an uncaught exception of type {} for task {}, with error message: {}\",\n   324→            exception.getClass().getName(),\n   325→            taskId,\n   326→            exception.getMessage());\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    public Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> drainUncaughtExceptions() {\n   330→        final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> returnValue = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   331→            final Map<TaskId, RuntimeException> result = new HashMap<>(uncaughtExceptions);\n   332→            uncaughtExceptions.clear();\n   333→            return result;\n   334→        });\n   335→\n   336→        if (!returnValue.isEmpty()) {\n   337→            log.debug(\"Drained {} uncaught exceptions\", returnValue.size());\n   338→        }\n   339→\n   340→        return returnValue;\n   341→    }\n   342→\n   343→    public boolean hasUncaughtException(final TaskId taskId) {\n   344→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> uncaughtExceptions.containsKey(taskId));\n   345→    }\n   346→\n   347→    private void executeWithTasksLocked(final Runnable action) {\n   348→        tasksLock.lock();\n   349→        try {\n   350→            action.run();\n   351→        } finally {\n   352→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   353→        }\n   354→    }\n   355→\n   356→    private <T> T returnWithTasksLocked(final Supplier<T> action) {\n   357→        tasksLock.lock();\n   358→        try {\n   359→            return action.get();\n   360→        } finally {\n   361→            tasksLock.unlock();\n   362→        }\n   363→    }\n   364→\n   365→    private boolean canProgress(final StreamTask task, final long nowMs) {\n   366→        return\n   367→            taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(task, nowMs) && task.isProcessable(nowMs) ||\n   368→                taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(task) && (task.canPunctuateStreamTime() || task.canPunctuateSystemTime());\n   369→    }\n   370→\n   371→    public void startTaskExecutors() {\n   372→ 107→import static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ClientUtils.restoreConsumerClientId;\n   108→\n   109→@SuppressWarnings(\"ClassDataAbstractionCoupling\")\n   110→public class StreamThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n   111→\n   112→    private static final String THREAD_ID_SUBSTRING = \"-StreamThread-\";\n   113→    private static final String STATE_UPDATER_ID_SUBSTRING = \"-StateUpdater-\";\n   114→\n   115→    /**\n   116→     * Stream thread states are the possible states that a stream thread can be in.\n   117→     * A thread must only be in one state at a time\n   118→     * The expected state transitions with the following defined states is:\n   119→     *\n   120→     * <pre>\n   121→     *                 +-------------+\n   122→     *          +<---- | Created (0) |\n   123→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   124→     *          |            |\n   125→     *          |            v\n   126→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   127→     *          +<---- | Starting (1)|----->+\n   128→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   129→     *          |                           |\n   130→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   131→     *          |            |           |  |\n   132→     *          |            v           |  |\n   133→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   134→     *          +<---- | Partitions  | --+  |\n   135→     *          |      | Revoked (2) | <----+\n   136→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   137→     *          |           |  ^            |\n   138→     *          |           v  |            |\n   139→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   140→     *          +<---- | Partitions  |      |\n   141→     *          |      | Assigned (3)| <----+\n   142→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   143→     *          |            |              |\n   144→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   145→     *          |            |           |  |\n   146→     *          |            v           |  |\n   147→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   148→     *          |      |             | --+  |\n   149→     *          |      | Running (4) | ---->+\n   150→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   151→     *          |            |\n   152→     *          |            v\n   153→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   154→     *          +----> | Pending     |\n   155→     *                 | Shutdown (5)|\n   156→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   157→     *                       |\n   158→     *                       v\n   159→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   160→     *                 | Dead (6)    |\n   161→     *                 +-------------+\n   162→     * </pre>\n   163→     *\n   164→     * Note the following:\n   165→     * <ul>\n   166→     *     <li>Any state can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN. That is because streams can be closed at any time.</li>\n   167→     *     <li>\n   168→     *         State PENDING_SHUTDOWN may want to transit to some other states other than DEAD,\n   169→     *         in the corner case when the shutdown is triggered while the thread is still in the rebalance loop.\n   170→     *         In this case we will forbid the transition but will not treat as an error.\n   171→     *     </li>\n   172→     *     <li>\n   173→     *         State PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want transit to itself indefinitely, in the corner case when\n   174→     *         the coordinator repeatedly fails in-between revoking partitions and assigning new partitions.\n   175→     *         Also during streams instance start up PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want to transit to itself as well.\n   176→     *         In this case we will allow the transition but it will be a no-op as the set of revoked partitions\n   177→     *         should be empty.\n   178→     *     </li>\n   179→     * </ul>\n   180→     */\n   181→    public enum State implements ThreadStateTransitionValidator {\n   182→\n   183→        CREATED(1, 5),                    // 0\n   184→        STARTING(2, 3, 5),                // 1\n   185→        PARTITIONS_REVOKED(2, 3, 5),      // 2\n   186→        PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED(2, 3, 4, 5),  // 3\n   187→        RUNNING(2, 3, 4, 5),              // 4\n   188→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(6),              // 5\n   189→        DEAD;                             // 6\n   190→\n   191→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   192→\n   193→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   194→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        public boolean isAlive() {\n   198→            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(STARTING) || equals(PARTITIONS_REVOKED) || equals(PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED);\n   199→        }\n   200→\n   201→        @Override\n   202→        public boolean isValidTransition(final ThreadStateTransitionValidator newState) {\n   203→            final State tmpState = (State) newState;\n   204→            return validTransitions.contains(tmpState.ordinal());\n   205→        }\n   206→    }\n   207→\n   208→    /**\n   209→     * Listen to state change events\n   210→     */\n   211→    public interface StateListener {\n   212→\n   213→        /**\n   214→         * Called when state changes\n   215→         *\n   216→         * @param thread   thread changing state\n   217→         * @param newState current state\n   218→         * @param oldState previous state\n   219→         */\n   220→        void onChange(final Thread thread, final ThreadStateTransitionValidator newState, final ThreadStateTransitionValidator oldState);\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * Set the {@link StreamThread.StateListener} to be notified when state changes. StreamThread.StateListener listener) {\n   228→        stateListener = listener;\n   229→    }\n   230→\n   231→    public StreamThread.StateListener getStateListener() {\n   232→        return stateListener;\n   233→    }\n   234→\n   235→    /**\n   236→     * @return The state this instance is in\n   237→     */\n   238→    public State state() {\n   239→        // we do not need to use the state lock since the variable is volatile\n   240→        return state;\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    void setPartitionAssignedTime(final long lastPartitionAssignedMs) {\n   244→        this.lastPartitionAssignedMs = lastPartitionAssignedMs;\n   245→    }\n   246→\n   247→    /**\n   248→     * Sets the state\n   249→     *\n   250→     * @param newState New state\n   251→     * @return The state prior to the call to setState, or null if the transition is invalid\n   252→     */\n   253→    State setState(final State newState) {\n   254→        final State oldState;\n   255→\n   256→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   257→            oldState = state;\n   258→\n   259→            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState != State.DEAD) {\n   260→                log.debug(\"Ignoring request to transit from PENDING_SHUTDOWN to {}: \" +\n   261→                              \"only DEAD state is a valid next state\", newState);\n   262→                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, all other transitions will be\n   263→                // refused but we do not throw exception here\n   264→                return null;\n   265→            } else if (state == State.DEAD) {\n   266→                log.debug(\"Ignoring request to transit from DEAD to {}: \" +\n   267→                              \"no valid next state after DEAD\", newState);\n   268→                // when the state is already in NOT_RUNNING, all its transitions\n   269→                // will be refused but we do not throw exception here\n   270→                return null;\n   271→            } else if (!state.isValidTransition(newState)) {\n   272→                log.error(\"Unexpected state transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   273→                throw new StreamsException(logPrefix + \"Unexpected state transition from \" + oldState + \" to \" + newState);\n   274→            } else {\n   275→                log.info(\"State transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   276→            }\n   277→\n   278→            state = newState;\n   279→            if (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n   280→                updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());\n   281→            }\n   282→\n   283→            stateLock.notifyAll();\n   284→        }\n   285→\n   286→        if (stateListener != null) {\n   287→            stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);\n   288→        }\n   289→\n   290→        return oldState;\n   291→    }\n   292→\n   293→    public isStartingRunningOrPartitionAssigned() {\n   300→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   301→            return state.equals(State.RUNNING) || state.equals(State.STARTING) || org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TopologyMetadata.UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY;\n   119→\n   120→/**\n   121→ * A Kafka client that allows for performing continuous computation on input coming from one or more input topics and\n   122→ * sends output to zero, one, or more output topics.\n   123→ * <p>\n   124→ * The computational logic can be specified either by using the {@link Topology} to define a DAG topology of\n   125→ * {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.api.Processor}s or by using the {@link StreamsBuilder} which provides the high-level DSL to define\n   126→ * transformations.\n   127→ * <p>\n   128→ * One {@code KafkaStreams} instance can contain one or more threads specified in the configs for the processing work.\n   129→ * <p>\n   130→ * A {@code KafkaStreams} instance can co-ordinate with any other instances with the same\n   131→ * {@link StreamsConfig#APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG application ID} (whether in the same process, on other processes on this\n   132→ * machine, or on remote machines) as a single (possibly distributed) stream processing application.\n   133→ * These instances will divide up the work based on the assignment of the input topic partitions so that all partitions\n   134→ * are being consumed.\n   135→ * If instances are added or fail, all (remaining) instances will rebalance the partition assignment among themselves\n   136→ * to balance processing load and ensure that all input topic partitions are processed.\n   137→ * <p>\n   138→ * Internally a {@code KafkaStreams} instance contains a normal {@link KafkaProducer} and {@link KafkaConsumer} instance\n   139→ * that is used for reading input and writing output.\n   140→ * <p>\n   141→ * A simple example might look like this:\n   142→ * <pre>{@code\n   143→ * Properties props = new Properties();\n   144→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG, \"my-stream-processing-application\");\n   145→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n   146→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_KEY_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n   147→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_VALUE_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n   148→ *\n   149→ * StreamsBuilder builder = new StreamsBuilder();\n   150→ * builder.<String, String>stream(\"my-input-topic\").mapValues(value -> String.valueOf(value.length())).to(\"my-output-topic\");\n   151→ *\n   152→ * KafkaStreams streams = new KafkaStreams(builder.build(), props);\n   153→ * streams.start();\n   154→ * }</pre>\n   155→ *\n   156→ * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.StreamsBuilder\n   157→ * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.Topology\n   158→ */\n   159→public class private final Object changeThreadCount = new Object();\n   193→\n   194→    // container states\n   195→    /**\n   196→     * Kafka Streams states are the possible state that a Kafka Streams instance can be in.\n   197→     * An instance must only be in one state at a time.\n   198→     * The expected state transition with the following defined states is:\n   199→     *\n   200→     * <pre>\n   201→     *                 +--------------+\n   202→     *         +&lt;----- | Created (0)  |\n   203→     *         |       +-----+--------+\n   204→     *         |             |\n   205→     *         |             v\n   206→     *         |       +----+--+------+\n   207→     *         |       | Re-          |\n   208→     *         +&lt;----- | Balancing (1)| --------&gt;+\n   209→     *         |       +-----+-+------+          |\n   210→     *         |             | ^                 |\n   211→     *         |             v |                 |\n   212→     *         |       +--------------+          v\n   213→     *         |       | Running (2)  | --------&gt;+\n   214→     *         |       +------+-------+          |\n   215→     *         |              |                  |\n   216→     *         |              v                  |\n   217→     *         |       +------+-------+     +----+-------+\n   218→     *         +-----&gt; | Pending      |     | Pending    |\n   219→     *                 | Shutdown (3) |     | Error (5)  |\n   220→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+------+\n   221→     *                        |                   |\n   222→     *                        v                   v\n   223→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+--------+\n   224→     *                 | Not          |     | Error (6)    |\n   225→     *                 | Running (4)  |     +--------------+\n   226→     *                 +--------------+\n   227→     *\n   228→     *\n   229→     * </pre>\n   230→     * Note the following:\n   231→     * <ul>\n   232→     *   <li>\n   233→     *     RUNNING state will transit to REBALANCING if any of its threads is in PARTITION_REVOKED or PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED state\n   234→     *   </li>\n   235→     *   <li>\n   236→     *     REBALANCING state will transit to RUNNING if all of its threads are in RUNNING state\n   237→     *     (Note: a thread transits to RUNNING state, if all active tasks got restored are ready for processing.\n   238→     *     Standby tasks are not considered.)\n   239→     *   </li>\n   240→     *   <li>\n   241→     *     Any state except NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN (whenever close is called)\n   242→     *   </li>\n   243→     *   <li>\n   244→     *     PENDING_SHUTDOWN and PENDING_ERROR are transitory states where the Streams application gracefully closes\n   245→     *     its existing resources before transitioning into their corresponding terminal states. These states are\n   246→     *     not recoverable, and only a restart would get an application back to the RUNNING state.\n   247→     *   </li>\n   248→     *   <li>\n   249→     *     Of special importance: If the global stream thread dies, or all stream threads die (or both) then\n   250→     *     the instance will be in the ERROR state. The user will not need to close it.\n   251→     *   </li>\n   252→     * </ul>\n   253→     */\n   254→    public enum State {\n   255→        // Note: if you add a new state, check the below methods and how they are used within Streams to see if\n   256→        // any of them should be updated to include the new state. For example a new shutdown path or terminal\n   257→        // state would likely need to be included in methods like isShuttingDown(), hasCompletedShutdown(), etc.\n   258→        CREATED(1, 3),          // 0\n   259→        REBALANCING(2, 3, 5),   // 1\n   260→        RUNNING(1, 2, 3, 5),    // 2\n   261→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(4),    // 3\n   262→        NOT_RUNNING,            // 4\n   263→        PENDING_ERROR(6),       // 5\n   264→        ERROR;                  // 6\n   265→\n   266→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   267→\n   268→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   269→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   270→        }\n   271→\n   272→        public boolean hasNotStarted() {\n   273→            return equals(CREATED);\n   274→        }\n   275→\n   276→        public boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n   277→            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(REBALANCING);\n   278→        }\n   279→\n   280→        public boolean isShuttingDown() {\n   281→            return equals(PENDING_SHUTDOWN) || equals(PENDING_ERROR);\n   282→        }\n   283→\n   284→        public boolean hasCompletedShutdown() {\n   285→            return equals(NOT_RUNNING) || equals(ERROR);\n   286→        }\n   287→\n   288→        public boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n   289→            return isShuttingDown() || hasCompletedShutdown();\n   290→        }\n   291→\n   292→        public boolean isValidTransition(final State newState) {\n   293→            return validTransitions.contains(newState.ordinal());\n   294→        }\n   295→    }\n   296→\n   297→    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n   298→    protected volatile State state = State.CREATED;\n   299→\n   300→    private boolean waitOnStates(final long waitMs, final State... targetStates) {"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01FFZ4SQpTbNA4MBRmN1pa5Z","session_id":"795a3160-79e8-469a-af09-524d2fece595","uuid":"3a1285af-2e70-43e2-9420-2a5618009b16"}

There are cases\n            // when we don't have a global state thread at all, e.g., when we don't have global KTables\n            if (globalThreadState != null && globalThreadState != GlobalStreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                return;\n--\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void closeToError() {\n        if (!setState(State.PENDING_ERROR)) {\n            log.info(\"Skipping shutdown since we are already in {}\", state());\n        } else {\n            final Thread shutdownThread = shutdownHelper(true, -1, org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation.REMAIN_IN_GROUP);\n\n            shutdownThread.setDaemon(true);\n            shutdownThread.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Shutdown this {@code KafkaStreams} by signaling all the threads to stop, and then wait up to the timeout for the\n     * threads to join.\n     * A {@code timeout} of {@link Duration#ZERO} (or any other zero duration) makes the close operation asynchronous.\n     * Negative-duration timeouts are rejected.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01FFZ4SQpTbNA4MBRmN1pa5Z","session_id":"795a3160-79e8-469a-af09-524d2fece595","uuid":"aa918938-3bbe-4dd1-9eb4-384b42ca995c"}

Modifies builders (line 253)\n3. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n */\npublic final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n\n    private final Time time;\n    private final Logger log;\n    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n\n    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n\n    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n\n    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n        @Override\n        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n                              final String clientId,\n                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n                              final int numExecutors\n                              ) {\n        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n        this.time = time;\n        this.tasks = tasks;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n\n        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n            }\n\n            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n                ) {\n\n                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n\n                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n\n                    return task;\n                }\n            }\n\n            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());","numLines":120,"startLine":1,"totalLines":387}}}

When `StreamThread.setState()` is called with `newState == State.RUNNING`:\n\n1. `StreamThread.stateLock` is held (line 256)\n2. rebuildMetadataForNamedTopologies(activePartitionHostMap, standbyPartitionHostMap) :\n   359→            rebuildMetadataForSingleTopology(activePartitionHostMap, standbyPartitionHostMap);\n   360→    }\n   361→\n   362→    private List<StreamsMetadata> rebuildMetadataForNamedTopologies(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n   363→                                                                    final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap) {\n   364→        final List<StreamsMetadata> rebuiltMetadata = new ArrayList<>();\n   365→        Stream.concat(activePartitionHostMap.keySet().stream(), standbyPartitionHostMap.keySet().stream())\n   366→            .distinct()\n   367→            .sorted(Comparator.comparing(HostInfo::host).thenComparingInt(HostInfo::port))\n   368→            .forEach(hostInfo -> {\n   369→                for (final String topologyName : topologyMetadata.namedTopologiesView()) {\n   370→                    final Map<String, List<String>> storeToSourceTopics =\n   371→                        topologyMetadata.stateStoreNameToSourceTopicsForTopology(topologyName);\n   372→\n   373→                    final Set<TopicPartition> activePartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   374→                    final Set<String> activeStoresOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   375→                    if (activePartitionHostMap.containsKey(hostInfo)) {\n   376→                        // filter out partitions for topics that are not connected to this topology\n   377→                        activePartitionsOnHost.addAll(\n   378→                            activePartitionHostMap.get(hostInfo).stream()\n   379→                                .filter(tp -> topologyMetadata.fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(topologyName).contains(tp.topic()))\n   380→                                .collect(Collectors.toSet())\n   381→                        );\n   382→                        activeStoresOnHost.addAll(getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, activePartitionsOnHost));\n   383→                    }\n   384→\n   385→                    final Set<TopicPartition> standbyPartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   386→                    final Set<String> standbyStoresOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   387→                    if (standbyPartitionHostMap.containsKey(hostInfo)) {\n   388→                        standbyPartitionsOnHost.addAll(\n   389→                            standbyPartitionHostMap.get(hostInfo).stream()\n   390→                                .filter(tp -> topologyMetadata.fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(topologyName).contains(tp.topic()))\n   391→                                .collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n   392→                        standbyStoresOnHost.addAll(getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, standbyPartitionsOnHost));\n   393→                    }\n   394→\n   395→                    final StreamsMetadata metadata = new StreamsMetadataImpl(\n   396→                        hostInfo,\n   397→                        activeStoresOnHost,\n   398→                        activePartitionsOnHost,\n   399→                        standbyStoresOnHost,\n   400→                        standbyPartitionsOnHost,\n   401→                        topologyName\n   402→                    );\n   403→\n   404→                    rebuiltMetadata.add(metadata);\n   405→                    if (hostInfo.equals(thisHost)) {\n   406→                        localMetadata.set(metadata);\n   407→                    }\n   408→                }\n   409→\n   410→                // Construct metadata across all topologies on this host for the `localMetadata` field\n   411→                final Map<String, List<String>> storeToSourceTopics = topologyMetadata.stateStoreNameToSourceTopics();\n   412→                final Set<TopicPartition> localActivePartitions = activePartitionHostMap.get(thisHost);\n   413→                final Set<TopicPartition> localStandbyPartitions = standbyPartitionHostMap.get(thisHost);\n   414→\n   415→                localMetadata.set(\n   416→                    new StreamsMetadataImpl(thisHost,\n   417→                                            getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, localActivePartitions),\n   418→                                            localActivePartitions,\n   419→                                            getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, localStandbyPartitions),\n   420→                                            localStandbyPartitions)\n   421→                );\n   422→            });\n   423→        return rebuiltMetadata;\n   424→    }\n   425→\n   426→    private List<StreamsMetadata> rebuildMetadataForSingleTopology(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n   427→                                                                   final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap) {\n   428→        final List<StreamsMetadata> rebuiltMetadata = new ArrayList<>();\n   429→        final .forEach(hostInfo -> {\n   434→                final Set<TopicPartition> activePartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   435→                final Set<String> activeStoresOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   436→                if (activePartitionHostMap.containsKey(hostInfo)) {\n   437→                    activePartitionsOnHost.addAll(activePartitionHostMap.get(hostInfo));\n   438→                    activeStoresOnHost.addAll(getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, activePartitionsOnHost));\n   439→                }\n   440→                activeStoresOnHost.addAll(globalStores);\n   441→\n   442→                final Set<TopicPartition> standbyPartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   443→                final Set<String> standbyStoresOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n   444→                if 464→\n   465→    private final Function<Optional<Set<Integer>>, Integer> getPartition = maybeMulticastPartitions -> {\n   466→        if (maybeMulticastPartitions.isEmpty()) {\n   467→            return null;\n   468→        }\n   469→        if (maybeMulticastPartitions.get().size() != 1) {\n   470→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The partitions returned by StreamPartitioner#partitions method when used for fetching KeyQueryMetadata for key should be a singleton set\");\n   471→        }\n   472→        return maybeMulticastPartitions.get().iterator().next();\n   473→    };\n   474→\n   475→    private <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n   476→                                                        final K key,\n   477→                                                        final StreamPartitioner<? super K, ?> partitioner,\n   478→                                                        final SourceTopicsInfo sourceTopicsInfo) {\n   479→\n   480→        final Integer partition = getPartition.apply(partitioner.partitions(sourceTopicsInfo.topicWithMostPartitions, key, null, sourceTopicsInfo.maxPartitions));\n   481→        final Set<TopicPartition> matchingPartitions = new HashSet<>();\n   482→        for (final String sourceTopic : sourceTopicsInfo.sourceTopics) {\n   483→            matchingPartitions.add(new TopicPartition(sourceTopic, partition));\n   484→        }\n   485→\n   486→        HostInfo activeHost = UNKNOWN_HOST;\n   487→        final Set<HostInfo> standbyHosts = new HashSet<>();\n   488→        for (final StreamsMetadata streamsMetadata : allMetadata) {\n   489→            final Set<String> activeStateStoreNames = streamsMetadata.stateStoreNames();\n   490→            final Set<TopicPartition> topicPartitions = new HashSet<>(streamsMetadata.topicPartitions());\n   491→            final Set<String> standbyStateStoreNames = streamsMetadata.standbyStateStoreNames();\n   492→            final Set<TopicPartition> standbyTopicPartitions = new HashSet<>(streamsMetadata.standbyTopicPartitions());\n   493→\n   494→            topicPartitions.retainAll(matchingPartitions);\n   495→            if (activeStateStoreNames.contains(storeName) && !topicPartitions.isEmpty()) {\n   496→                activeHost = streamsMetadata.hostInfo();\n   497→            }\n   498→\n   499→            standbyTopicPartitions.retainAll(matchingPartitions);\n   500→            if (standbyStateStoreNames.contains(storeName) && !standbyTopicPartitions.isEmpty()) {\n   501→                standbyHosts.add(streamsMetadata.hostInfo());\n   502→            }\n   503→        }\n   504→\n   505→        return new KeyQueryMetadata(activeHost, standbyHosts, partition);\n   506→    }\n   507→\n   508→    private <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n   509→                                                        final K key,\n   510→                                                        final StreamPartitioner<? super K, ?> partitioner,\n   511→                                                        final SourceTopicsInfo sourceTopicsInfo,\n   512→                                                        final String topologyName) {\n   513→        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topology name must not be null\");\n   514→        final Integer partition = getPartition.apply(partitioner.partitions(sourceTopicsInfo.topicWithMostPartitions, key, null, sourceTopicsInfo.maxPartitions));\n   515→        final Set<TopicPartition> matchingPartitions = new HashSet<>();\n   516→        for (final String sourceTopic : sourceTopicsInfo.sourceTopics) {\n   517→            matchingPartitions.add(new TopicPartition(sourceTopic, partition));\n   518→        }\n   519→\n   520→        HostInfo activeHost = UNKNOWN_HOST;\n   521→        final Set<HostInfo> standbyHosts = new HashSet<>();\n   522→        for (final StreamsMetadata streamsMetadata : allMetadata) {\n   523→            final String             final Set<String> activeStateStoreNames = streamsMetadata.stateStoreNames();\n   527→                final Set<TopicPartition> topicPartitions = new HashSet<>(streamsMetadata.topicPartitions());\n   528→                final Set<String> standbyStateStoreNames = streamsMetadata.standbyStateStoreNames();\n   529→                final      if (activeStateStoreNames.contains(storeName) && !topicPartitions.isEmpty()) {\n   533→                    activeHost = streamsMetadata.hostInfo();\n   534→                }\n   535→\n   536→                standbyTopicPartitions.retainAll(matchingPartitions);\n   537→                if (standbyStateStoreNames.contains(storeName) && !standbyTopicPartitions.isEmpty()) {\n   538→                    standbyHosts.add(streamsMetadata.hostInfo());\n   539→                }\n   540→            }\n   541→        }\n   542→\n   543→        return new KeyQueryMetadata(activeHost, standbyHosts, partition);\n   544→    }\n   545→\n   546→    private SourceTopicsInfo getSourceTopicsInfo(final String storeName) {\n   547→        return getSourceTopicsInfo(storeName, null);\n   548→    }\n   549→\n   550→    private SourceTopicsInfo getSourceTopicsInfo(final String storeName, final String topologyName) {\n   551→        final List<String> sourceTopics = new ArrayList<>(topologyMetadata.sourceTopicsForStore(storeName, topologyName));\n   552→\n   553→        if (sourceTopics.isEmpty()) {\n   554→            return null;\n   555→        }\n   556→        return new SourceTopicsInfo(sourceTopics);\n   557→    }\n   558→\n   559→    private boolean isInitialized() {\n   560→        return partitionsByTopic != null && !partitionsByTopic.isEmpty() && localMetadata.get() != null;\n   561→    }\n   562→\n   563→    public String storeForChangelogTopic(final String topicName) {\n   564→        return topologyMetadata.storeForChangelogTopic(topicName);\n   565→    }\n   566→\n   567→    private class SourceTopicsInfo {\n   568→        private final List<String> sourceTopics;\n   569→        private int maxPartitions;\n   570→        private String topicWithMostPartitions;\n   571→\n   572→        private SourceTopicsInfo(final List<String> sourceTopics) {\n   573→            this.sourceTopics = sourceTopics;\n   574→            for (final String topic : sourceTopics) {\n   575→                final List<PartitionInfo> partitions = partitionsByTopic.getOrDefault(topic, Collections.emptyList());\n   576→                if (partitions.size() > maxPartitions) {\n   577→                    maxPartitions = partitions.size();\n   578→                    topicWithMostPartitions = topic;\n   579→                }\n   580→            }\n   581→        }\n   582→    }\n   583→}\n This can be used\n * to discover the locations of {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.StateStore}s\n * in a KafkaStreams application\n */\npublic class StreamsMetadataState {\n    private final Logger log;\n    public static final HostInfo UNKNOWN_HOST = HostInfo.unavailable();\n    private final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n    private final Set<String> globalStores;\n    private final HostInfo thisHost;\n    private List<StreamsMetadata> allMetadata = Collections.emptyList();\n    private Map<String, List<PartitionInfo>> partitionsByTopic;\n    private final AtomicReference<StreamsMetadata> localMetadata = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n\n    public StreamsMetadataState(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n                                final HostInfo thisHost,\n                                final LogContext logContext) {\n        this.topologyMetadata = topologyMetadata;\n        this.globalStores = this.topologyMetadata.globalStateStores().keySet();\n        this.thisHost = thisHost;\n        this.log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return toString(\"\");\n    }\n\n    public String toString(final String indent) {\n        return indent + \"GlobalMetadata: \" + allMetadata + \"\\n\" +\n                indent + \"GlobalStores: \" + globalStores + \"\\n\" +\n                indent + \"My HostInfo: \" + thisHost + \"\\n\" +\n                indent + \"PartitionsByTopic: \" + partitionsByTopic + \"\\n\";\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the {@link StreamsMetadata}s for the local instance in a {@link KafkaStreams application}\n     *\n     * @return the {@link StreamsMetadata}s for the local instance in a {@link KafkaStreams} application\n     */\n    public StreamsMetadata getLocalMetadata() {\n        return localMetadata.get();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find all of the {@link StreamsMetadata}s in a\n     * {@link KafkaStreams application}\n     *\n     * @return all the {@link StreamsMetadata}s in a {@link KafkaStreams} application\n     */\n    public Collection<StreamsMetadata> allMetadata() {\n        return Collections.unmodifiableList(allMetadata);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find all of the {@link StreamsMetadata}s for a given storeName\n     *\n     * @param storeName the storeName to find metadata for\n     * @return A collection of {@link StreamsMetadata} that have the provided storeName\n     */\n    public synchronized Collection<StreamsMetadata> allMetadataForStore(final String storeName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(storeName, \"storeName cannot be null\");\n        if (topologyMetadata.hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Cannot invoke the allMetadataForStore(storeName) method when\"\n                                                   + \"using named topologies, please use the overload that accepts\"\n                                                   + \"a topologyName parameter to identify the correct store\");\n        }\n\n        if (!isInitialized()) {\n            return Collections.emptyList();\n        }\n\n        if (globalStores.contains(storeName)) {\n            return allMetadata;\n        }\n\n        final Collection<String> sourceTopics = topologyMetadata.sourceTopicsForStore(storeName, null);\n        if (sourceTopics.isEmpty()) {\n            return Collections.emptyList();\n        }\n\n        final ArrayList<StreamsMetadata> results = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (final StreamsMetadata metadata : allMetadata) {\n            if (metadata.stateStoreNames().contains(storeName) || metadata.standbyStateStoreNames().contains(storeName)) {\n                results.add(metadata);\n            }\n        }\n        return results;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find all of the {@link StreamsMetadata}s for a given storeName in the given topology\n     *\n     * @param storeName the storeName to find metadata for\n     * @param topologyName the storeName to find metadata for\n     * @return A collection of {@link StreamsMetadata} that have the provided storeName\n     */\n    public synchronized Collection<StreamsMetadata> allMetadataForStore(final String storeName, final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(storeName, \"storeName cannot be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topologyName cannot be null\");\n\n        if (!isInitialized()) {\n            return Collections.emptyList();\n        }\n\n        final Collection<String> sourceTopics = topologyMetadata.sourceTopicsForStore(storeName, topologyName);\n        if (sourceTopics.isEmpty()) {\n            return Collections.emptyList();\n        }\n\n        final ArrayList<StreamsMetadata> results = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (final StreamsMetadata metadata : allMetadata) {\n            final String metadataTopologyName = ((StreamsMetadataImpl) metadata).topologyName();\n            if (metadataTopologyName != null && metadataTopologyName.equals(topologyName)\n                && metadata.stateStoreNames().contains(storeName) || metadata.standbyStateStoreNames().contains(storeName)) {\n                results.add(metadata);\n            }\n        }\n        return results;\n    }\n\n    public synchronized Collection<StreamsMetadata> allMetadataForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topologyName cannot be null\");\n\n        if (!isInitialized()) {\n            return Collections.emptyList();\n        }\n\n        final ArrayList<StreamsMetadata> results = new ArrayList<>();\n        for (final StreamsMetadata metadata : allMetadata) {\n            final String metadataTopologyName = ((StreamsMetadataImpl) metadata).topologyName();\n            if (metadataTopologyName != null && metadataTopologyName.equals(topologyName)) {\n                results.add(metadata);\n            }\n        }\n        return results;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find the {@link KeyQueryMetadata}s for a given storeName and key. This method will use the\n     * {@link DefaultStreamPartitioner} to locate the store. If a custom partitioner has been used\n     * please use {@link StreamsMetadataState#keyQueryMetadataForKey(String, Object, StreamPartitioner)} instead.\n     *\n     * Note: the key may not exist in the {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.StateStore},\n     * this method provides a way of finding which {@link KeyQueryMetadata} it would exist on.\n     *\n     * @param storeName     Name of the store\n     * @param key           Key to use\n     * @param keySerializer Serializer for the key\n     * @param <K>           key type\n     * @return The {@link KeyQueryMetadata} for the storeName and key or {@link KeyQueryMetadata#NOT_AVAILABLE}\n     * if streams is (re-)initializing or {@code null} if the corresponding topic cannot be found,\n     * or null if no matching metadata could be found.\n     */\n    public synchronized <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n                                                                    final K key,\n                                                                    final Serializer<K> keySerializer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(keySerializer, \"keySerializer can't be null\");\n        if (topologyMetadata.hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Cannot invoke the KeyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName, key, keySerializer)\"\n                                                   + \"method when using named topologies, please use the overload that\"\n                                                   + \"accepts a topologyName parameter to identify the correct store\");\n        }\n        return keyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName,\n                                      key,\n                                      new DefaultStreamPartitioner<>(keySerializer));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * See {@link StreamsMetadataState#keyQueryMetadataForKey(String, Object, Serializer)}\n     */\n    public synchronized <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n                                                                    final K key,\n                                                                    final Serializer<K> keySerializer,\n                                                                    final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(keySerializer, \"keySerializer can't be null\");\n        return keyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName,\n                                      key,\n                                      new DefaultStreamPartitioner<>(keySerializer),\n                                      topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find the {@link KeyQueryMetadata}s for a given storeName and key\n     *\n     * Note: the key may not exist in the {@link StateStore},this method provides a way of finding which\n     * {@link StreamsMetadata} it would exist on.\n     *\n     * @param storeName   Name of the store\n     * @param key         Key to use\n     * @param partitioner partitioner to use to find correct partition for key\n     * @param <K>         key type\n     * @return The {@link KeyQueryMetadata} for the storeName and key or {@link KeyQueryMetadata#NOT_AVAILABLE}\n     * if streams is (re-)initializing, or {@code null} if no matching metadata could be found.\n     */\n    public synchronized <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n                                                                    final K key,\n                                                                    final StreamPartitioner<? super K, ?> partitioner) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(storeName, \"storeName can't be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key can't be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(partitioner, \"partitioner can't be null\");\n        if (topologyMetadata.hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Cannot invoke the keyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName, key, partitioner)\"\n                                                   + \"method when using named topologies, please use the overload that\"\n                                                   + \"accepts a topologyName parameter to identify the correct store\");\n        }\n\n        if (!isInitialized()) {\n            return KeyQueryMetadata.NOT_AVAILABLE;\n        }\n\n        if (globalStores.contains(storeName)) {\n            // global stores are on every node. if we don't have the host info\n            // for this host then just pick the first metadata\n            if (thisHost.equals(UNKNOWN_HOST)) {\n                return new KeyQueryMetadata(allMetadata.get(0).hostInfo(), Collections.emptySet(), UNKNOWN_PARTITION);\n            }\n            return new KeyQueryMetadata(localMetadata.get().hostInfo(), Collections.emptySet(), UNKNOWN_PARTITION);\n        }\n\n        final SourceTopicsInfo sourceTopicsInfo = getSourceTopicsInfo(storeName);\n        if (sourceTopicsInfo == null) {\n            return null;\n        }\n        return keyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName, key, partitioner, sourceTopicsInfo);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * See {@link StreamsMetadataState#keyQueryMetadataForKey(String, Object, StreamPartitioner)}\n     */\n    public synchronized <K> KeyQueryMetadata keyQueryMetadataForKey(final String storeName,\n                                                                    final K key,\n                                                                    final StreamPartitioner<? super K, ?> partitioner,\n                                                                    final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(storeName, \"storeName can't be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(key, \"key can't be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(partitioner, \"partitioner can't be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topologyName can't be null\");\n\n\n        if (!isInitialized()) {\n            return KeyQueryMetadata.NOT_AVAILABLE;\n        }\n\n        final SourceTopicsInfo sourceTopicsInfo = getSourceTopicsInfo(storeName, topologyName);\n        if (sourceTopicsInfo == null) {\n            return null;\n        }\n        return keyQueryMetadataForKey(storeName, key, partitioner, sourceTopicsInfo, topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Respond to changes to the HostInfo -> TopicPartition mapping. Will rebuild the\n     * metadata\n     *\n     * @param activePartitionHostMap  the current mapping of {@link HostInfo} -> {@link TopicPartition}s for active partitions\n     * @param standbyPartitionHostMap the current mapping of {@link HostInfo} -> {@link TopicPartition}s for standby partitions\n     * @param topicPartitionInfo      the current mapping of {@link TopicPartition} -> {@Link PartitionInfo}\n     */\n    synchronized void onChange(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n                               final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap,\n                               final Map<TopicPartition, PartitionInfo> topicPartitionInfo) {\n        this.partitionsByTopic = new HashMap<>();\n        topicPartitionInfo.forEach((key, value) -> this.partitionsByTopic\n                .computeIfAbsent(key.topic(), topic -> new ArrayList<>())\n                .add(value));\n\n        rebuildMetadata(activePartitionHostMap, standbyPartitionHostMap);\n    }\n\n    private boolean hasPartitionsForAnyTopics(final List<String> topicNames, final Set<TopicPartition> partitionForHost) {\n        for (final TopicPartition topicPartition : partitionForHost) {\n            if (topicNames.contains(topicPartition.topic())) {\n                return true;\n            }\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    private Set<String> getStoresOnHost(final Map<String, List<String>> storeToSourceTopics,\n                                        final Set<TopicPartition> sourceTopicPartitions) {\n        final Set<String> storesOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n        for (final Map.Entry<String, List<String>> storeTopicEntry : storeToSourceTopics.entrySet()) {\n            final List<String> topicsForStore = storeTopicEntry.getValue();\n            if (hasPartitionsForAnyTopics(topicsForStore, sourceTopicPartitions)) {\n                storesOnHost.add(storeTopicEntry.getKey());\n            }\n        }\n        return storesOnHost;\n    }\n\n    private void rebuildMetadata(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n                                 final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap) {\n        if (activePartitionHostMap.isEmpty() && standbyPartitionHostMap.isEmpty()) {\n            allMetadata = Collections.emptyList();\n            localMetadata.set(new StreamsMetadataImpl(\n                thisHost,\n                Collections.emptySet(),\n                Collections.emptySet(),\n                Collections.emptySet(),\n                Collections.emptySet()\n            ));\n            return;\n        }\n\n        allMetadata = topologyMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() ?\n            rebuildMetadataForNamedTopologies(activePartitionHostMap, standbyPartitionHostMap) :\n            rebuildMetadataForSingleTopology(activePartitionHostMap, standbyPartitionHostMap);\n    }\n\n    private List<StreamsMetadata> rebuildMetadataForNamedTopologies(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n                                                                    final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap) {\n        final List<StreamsMetadata> rebuiltMetadata = new ArrayList<>();\n        Stream.concat(activePartitionHostMap.keySet().stream(), standbyPartitionHostMap.keySet().stream())\n            .distinct()\n            .sorted(Comparator.comparing(HostInfo::host).thenComparingInt(HostInfo::port))\n            .forEach(hostInfo -> {\n                for (final String topologyName : topologyMetadata.namedTopologiesView()) {\n                    final Map<String, List<String>> storeToSourceTopics =\n                        topologyMetadata.stateStoreNameToSourceTopicsForTopology(topologyName);\n\n                    final Set<TopicPartition> activePartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n                    final connected to this topology\n                        activePartitionsOnHost.addAll(\n                            activePartitionHostMap.get(hostInfo).stream()\n                                .filter(tp -> topologyMetadata.fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(topologyName).contains(tp.topic()))\n                                .collect(Collectors.toSet())\n                        );\n                        activeStoresOnHost.addAll(getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, activePartitionsOnHost));\n                    }\n\n                    final Set<TopicPartition> standbyPartitionsOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n                    final Set<String> standbyStoresOnHost = new HashSet<>();\n                    if (standbyPartitionHostMap.containsKey(hostInfo)) {\n topologyMetadata.fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(topologyName).contains(tp.topic()))\n                                .collect(Collectors.toSet()));\n                        standbyStoresOnHost.addAll(getStoresOnHost(storeToSourceTopics, standbyPartitionsOnHost));\n                    }\n\n                    final StreamsMetadata metadata = new StreamsMetadataImpl(\n                        hostInfo,\n                        activeStoresOnHost,\n                        activePartitionsOnHost,\n                        standbyStoresOnHost,\n                        standbyPartitionsOnHost,\n                        topologyName\n                    );\n\n                    rebuiltMetadata.add(metadata);\n                    if (hostInfo.equals(thisHost)) {\n                        localMetadata.set(metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Construct metadata across all topologies on this host for the `localMetadata` field\n activePartitionHostMap.get(thisHost);\n                final Set<TopicPartition> localStandbyPartitions = standbyPartitionHostMap.get(thisHost);\n\n                localMetadata.set(\n                );\n            });\n        return rebuiltMetadata;\n    }\n\n    private List<StreamsMetadata> rebuildMetadataForSingleTopology(final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> activePartitionHostMap,\n                                                                   final Map<HostInfo, Set<TopicPartition>> standbyPartitionHostMap) {\n        final List<StreamsMetadata> {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01SH4gceNZtkxWHFqMbzMo4A","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I found something very interesting! At line 1979:\n\n```java\npublic Set<ThreadMetadata> metadataForLocalThreads() {\n    final Set<ThreadMetadata> threadMetadata = new HashSet<>();\n    processStreamThread(thread -> {\n        synchronized (thread.getStateLock()) {\n            if (thread.state() != StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                threadMetadata.add(thread.threadMetadata());\n            }\n        }\n    });    return threadMetadata;}\n```\n\nThis method, `metadataForLocalThreads()`, acquires `StreamThread.stateLock` for each thread. And it's called from `processStreamThread`, which iterates all threads.\n\n\nI'm seeing a potential deadlock scenario: if `metadataForLocalThreads()` is called while holding another lock, and a StreamThread's `setState()` holds its state lock while trying to acquire that same other lock, we'd have a classic lock ordering violation. The tricky part is that `metadataForLocalThreads()` is a public API, so callers might not realize they're holding a lock when they invoke it. Looking at what happens in `setState()` when transitioning to the RUNNING state...\n\nExamining the state transition logic... Looking at `numLiveStreamThreads()`, it acquires the `threads` monitor and calls `thread.state()` which just reads a volatile variable without needing the state lock. The `processStreamThread` method creates a snapshot of the threads list and iterates over it, so there's no concurrent modification issue there either.\n\nLet me focus on the actual deadlock culprit: in `StreamThread.setState()`, I'm holding `stateLock` while calling `taskManager.activeTaskMap()`, which internally acquires another lock. Neither of these states triggers `updateThreadMetadata` (which only runs for `State.RUNNING`). So no stateUpdater locks are acquired from within `stateLock` during shutdown.\n\nLet me now try a totally different theory. Let me look at this specific code path very carefully:\n\nIn `StreamThread.setState(RUNNING)`:\n```java\nsynchronized (stateLock) {    // L5    state = newState;    if (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n        updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());        // taskManager.activeTaskMap() calls stateUpdater.tasks() which acquires L6+L7+L8    }\n\n\n```\n\nSo thread order: L5 → L6+L7+L8 (released) → L5 released → L3 → L2.\n\nNow, `StreamStateListener.onChange()` is synchronized (L3). Inside, it updates the thread state map and checks for partition revocation conditions...\n\nFor `newState == RUNNING`, it calls `maybeSetRunning()` which triggers `KafkaStreams.setState(RUNNING)`, acquiring L2. Since L2 is already released before the user listener runs, there's no immediate deadlock—the user listener executes without holding L2, then acquires L4 and L3. But this does create a potential cycle in the lock graph: L3 → L2 → (user listener) → L4 → L3.\n\nLet me trace through what happens when the user's listener calls `addStreamThread()`: it first checks `isRunningOrRebalancing()` which briefly acquires and releases L2, then acquires L4 for the synchronized block, and inside that calls `createAndAddStreamThread()` which triggers `streamStateListener.registerStreamThread()` to acquire L3. So the path is L4 → L3. Meanwhile, Thread A holding L3 in `StreamStateListener.onChange()` calls `setState()` to acquire L2, creating the L3 → L2 link. The close thread adds L1 → L2. I'll create a test that exercises the StreamThread and KafkaStreams interaction with multiple threads acquiring locks in different orders, and let Fray identify the actual deadlock.\n\nActually, let me start fresh and carefully examine what `StreamThread.setState()` does when transitioning to RUNNING...\n\nExamining the state transition code... Looking at the lock acquisition order here—when `KafkaStreams.setState(State.RUNNING)` gets called from within `StreamStateListener.onChange()`, it's holding lock L3 and then acquires L2, creating a potential L3 → L2 ordering. I need to check if there's anywhere else in the code that acquires these locks in the opposite order (L2 → L3), which would create a deadlock cycle.\n\nLet me trace through `addStreamThread()` to see what locks it acquires and in what order, since that's called during state transitions and might be the culprit.\n\nLooking at the code, `addStreamThread()` first checks if the streams are running, then acquires the `changeThreadCount` lock where it creates a new stream thread. Inside that synchronized block, it calls `createAndAddStreamThread()` which internally calls `streamStateListener.registerStreamThread()` — that's where L3 gets acquired. After releasing `changeThreadCount`, it then acquires the `stateLock` (L2) to start the thread. So the lock order here is L4 → L3, then later L2.\n\nNow I need to check `removeStreamThread()` to see if it has a different lock ordering that could create a deadlock scenario. So the issue is that `removeStreamThread()` holds L4 while calling `shutdown()`, which acquires L5 then L3 sequentially, and then `waitOnThreadState()` tries to acquire L5 while L4 is still held. Meanwhile, the StreamThread itself is running independently and could be trying to acquire these locks in a different order, creating a potential deadlock scenario. it shuts down, its `completeShutdown()` calls `setState(DEAD)`. `setState(DEAD)`:\n```java\nsynchronized (stateLock) {  // L5\n    state = DEAD;\n    stateLock.notifyAll();  // wakes up waitOnThreadState()\n}\nif (stateListener != null) {\n    stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);  // → StreamStateListener.onChange() → L3\n}\n```\n\nSo the StreamThread acquires L5 then L3 (sequentially). The `waitOnThreadState()` in `removeStreamThread()` holds L5 (via wait()), and `notifyAll()` wakes it up. After waking, it checks state, releases L5.\n\nBut during `removeStreamThread()`, L4 is held throughout. And when `StreamStateListener.onChange()` (L3) gets called from within L5 on the StreamThread, it's actually the calling thread that's executing this—the one holding L4. So on the calling thread, the lock acquisition order becomes L5 → L3, but L4 is already held, creating a potential deadlock if another thread tries to acquire these locks in a different order. So the calling thread acquires L3 while already holding L4, creating the path L4 → L3. After `shutdown()` returns, it waits on L5 (the StreamThread's stateLock), so now it's holding L4 and waiting on L5. Meanwhile, the StreamThread itself might be holding L5 while calling into other code that needs L6, L7, or L8—and since both threads are contending for the same L5, there's potential for a deadlock if the StreamThread needs to acquire L3 or L4 while the calling thread is waiting. calling thread blocks.\n\nBut that's not a deadlock - it's just contention. The StreamThread will eventually release L5.\n\nOK but here's a potential scenario:**Thread A** (calling removeStreamThread): holds L4 → acquires L3 (via shutdown/onChange) → releases L3 → acquires L5 (waitOnThreadState, waiting)**Thread B** (StreamThread transitioning to RUNNING): acquires L5 → calls stateUpdater.tasks() → acquires L6/L7/L8 → releases all → releases L5 → calls stateListener.onChange() → tries to acquire L3**Thread C** (another user thread calling addStreamThread): tries to acquire L4\n\nThat's still not a cycle. Thread A holds L4 and waits on L5. Thread B holds L5 and needs L3. Thread C needs L4. But let me trace through a tighter timing scenario: Thread B acquires L5 while transitioning to RUNNING, then Thread A tries to acquire L5 in `waitOnThreadState()` and blocks. Thread B calls `stateUpdater.tasks()` and releases those locks, finishes the state transition, releases L5. Thread A then acquires L5, sees the state is RUNNING not DEAD, and waits again.
